overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages may be unenforceable under Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may not owe 60× monthly royalty on termination

overrides.override_summary: Bus. & Prof. Code §§20000-20043 provide termination/non-renewal rights
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law may limit franchisor's ability to terminate or refuse relicensing

overrides.override_summary: NY governing law provision may not be enforceable in CA
overrides.why_it_matters: CA courts may apply CA law instead of NY law

overrides.override_summary: VA venue provision may not be enforceable in CA
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee may be able to litigate in CA

overrides.override_summary: General release cannot waive FIL rights (Corp. Code §31512, Bus. & Prof. §20010)
overrides.why_it_matters: Renewal/transfer release cannot extinguish CA franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: General release cannot relieve franchisor from HI franchise law liability
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor remains liable under HI law regardless of releases signed

overrides.override_summary: IL Franchise Disclosure Act (815 ILCS 705/19-20) governs termination/non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: IL law provides additional termination protections not in standard FA

overrides.override_summary: Governing law/venue/jury waiver cannot waive IL FDA rights
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees retain IL FDA rights regardless of NY choice of law

overrides.override_summary: MD law may supersede FA on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: MD termination protections may apply even if FA says otherwise

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may sue in Maryland for MD FRDL claims; 3-year SOL
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisee need not travel to VA to bring franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: General release cannot waive MD FRDL rights; anti-waiver on questionnaires/acknowledgments
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong protection against franchisor-imposed waivers

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause; 30-day cure minimum
overrides.why_it_matters: Prevents arbitrary termination; franchisee has cure opportunity

overrides.override_summary: Must renew on generally available terms; fair compensation on non-renewal (if <5yr term)
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor's ability to impose materially different terms on existing franchisees

overrides.override_summary: No out-of-state arbitration or litigation
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisee cannot be required to litigate in VA or NY

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require waiver of statutory rights as condition of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee from signing away MI franchise law protections

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages prohibited (MN Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees cannot be charged 60× monthly royalty on termination

overrides.override_summary: 90-day termination notice (60-day cure); 180-day non-renewal notice
overrides.why_it_matters: Much longer notice periods than standard FA (10-30 days)

overrides.override_summary: No out-of-state litigation; no jury trial waiver
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisee retains home-state litigation rights and jury trial right

overrides.override_summary: General release cannot relieve franchisor from MN FIL liability
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law claims survive any release

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees have statutory termination rights beyond FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: NY Franchises Law governs NY causes of action; GBL §§680-695 may supersede FA
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law provides additional protections not in FA

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal/transfer may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Release signed at renewal or transfer may be voidable

overrides.override_summary: ALL liquidated damages provisions deleted in entirety
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees owe zero liquidated damages on termination

overrides.override_summary: ND law supersedes NY law; FA governed by ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: Complete override of NY choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Out-of-state venue provisions deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisee cannot be forced to litigate in VA or NY

overrides.override_summary: No release can waive ND FDA liability
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise disclosure act claims survive any release

overrides.override_summary: Out-of-state venue and foreign governing law void for RI FIA claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisee can bring RI franchise law claims in RI under RI law

overrides.override_summary: Release on transfer/renewal does not waive RI FIA claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise investment act claims survive releases

overrides.override_summary: Cancellation without reasonable cause is unlawful (§13.1-564)
overrides.why_it_matters: VA Retail Franchising Act provides additional termination protection

overrides.override_summary: WA FIPA (RCW 19.100.180) may supersede FA on termination/renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: WA termination protections apply regardless of FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: Release limited — only via negotiated settlement with independent counsel after FA in effect
overrides.why_it_matters: Prevents form releases at signing, renewal, or transfer

overrides.override_summary: WA FIPA prevails in conflicts of law
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchise law overrides NY choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees limited to reasonable estimated or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: $200K COO fee may be challenged as exceeding actual costs

overrides.override_summary: Non-compete void unless employee >$100K/yr or IC >$250K/yr (RCW 49.62.020/030); no-poach provisions void (RCW 49.62.060)
overrides.why_it_matters: Effectively eliminates most non-compete and no-poach restrictions for WA franchisees
